name: Corned Beef (How to Cure Your Own)
recipeIngredient: 5 lb Boneless Beef Roast, about 10 inches long, 4 to 5 inches wide, and about 2 inches thick with a thin covering of fat on one side
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the meat in a suitable glass or plastic refrigerator container.
2. Mix all remaining ingredients together in a saucepan or bowl and pour over the meat.
3. Seal tightly with lid or plastic wrap, secured with a rubber band.
4. Refrigerate the meat mixture for 7 days. Turn the meat over in the marinade once a day on the 7th day.
5. Remove from the marinade and soak the meat in cold water, covered, draining it well.
6. Prepare for sandwiches by placing the drained, cured beef roast in an appropriate pot.
7. Add beer, Italian Dressing, and peppercorns; bring to boil.
8. Cover and reduce heat to gentle simmer for 2 1/2 hours.
9. Remove from the beer mixture. Add only 1 cup of the beer mixture to the beef in a shallow, small oblong roasting pan.
10. Seal with plastic wrap and refrigerate several hours or overnight.
11. Place in an oven preheated to 350 degrees F for 1 1/2 hours, sealing the pan in foil.
12. Cool and slice across grain for sandwiches. Slices are best when well chilled.
13. Freeze in family portions, sliced, to thaw and serve in 3 months. Refrigerate well-wrapped, to use in 4 or 5 days. Allow 1/2 lb per serving.
Optional Brine:
- Drain juice from bottled dill pickles (any kind), mixing in equal parts with Dry Vermouth, sufficient to submerge beef instead of the mixture given above.
